title: centaurea napulifera subsp. tuberosa
intro: Centaurea napulifera subsp. tuberosa, also known as the tuberous knapweed, is a plant species in the daisy family that has edible tubers and has been used in traditional medicine. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea napulifera subsp. tuberosa include its potential to treat various health conditions, such as digestive issues, skin irritations, and infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Centaurea napulifera subsp. tuberosa can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its tubers can be consumed raw or cooked, offering a natural and sustainable source of nutrition and medicinal properties.
